input: Patient: Jane Doe, 45F. Chief complaint: persistent chest tightness and shortness of breath for 3 days. PMH: hypertension, type 2 diabetes. Current medications: metformin 1000mg BID, lisinopril 10mg QD, atorvastatin 40mg QD. Vitals: BP 148/92, HR 88, SpO2 97%. EKG normal sinus rhythm. Assessment: likely musculoskeletal chest pain, GERD component possible. Plan: start omeprazole 20mg QD, follow up in 2 weeks; monitor BP closely.
reference: Jane Doe presents with chest tightness and shortness of breath. Current medications include metformin, lisinopril, and atorvastatin. Follow-up is scheduled in 2 weeks.
rubric: Summary must mention: chief complaint, current medications, and follow-up plan.

input: Patient: Robert Smith, 67M. CC: worsening knee pain, right knee, 6-week history. PMH: osteoarthritis bilateral knees, obesity BMI 34. Medications: acetaminophen 500mg PRN, ibuprofen 400mg BID as needed. Exam: crepitus right knee, mild effusion, ROM decreased 20 degrees. X-ray: moderate joint space narrowing. Plan: physical therapy referral, add meloxicam 15mg QD, reduce ibuprofen use, orthopedic consult if no improvement in 6 weeks.
reference: Robert Smith presents with worsening right knee pain secondary to osteoarthritis. Medications include acetaminophen and ibuprofen. Physical therapy was ordered and orthopedic consult is planned if no improvement in 6 weeks.
rubric: Summary must mention: chief complaint, current medications, and follow-up plan.

input: Patient: Maria Gonzalez, 32F. CC: severe migraine with photophobia and nausea, onset this morning. No aura. PMH: episodic migraine 2-3x per month. Current medications: sumatriptan 100mg PRN, oral contraceptives. Vitals stable. No papilledema. Assessment: acute migraine, no red flags. Plan: administer ketorolac 30mg IM in office, prescribe ondansetron 4mg ODT PRN nausea, review migraine triggers at next visit scheduled in 4 weeks.
reference: Maria Gonzalez presents with severe acute migraine with photophobia and nausea. Current medications include sumatriptan and oral contraceptives. Follow-up is scheduled in 4 weeks.
rubric: Summary must mention: chief complaint, current medications, and follow-up plan.

input: Patient: David Chen, 55M. CC: fatigue and unintentional weight loss of 12 lbs over 3 months. PMH: GERD, seasonal allergies. Meds: omeprazole 40mg QD, loratadine 10mg QD. Labs ordered: CBC, CMP, TSH, LDH, urine cytology. CT abdomen/pelvis ordered. Assessment: weight loss workup required, rule out malignancy. Follow-up: 1 week to review labs and imaging.
reference: David Chen presents with fatigue and unintentional 12-lb weight loss over 3 months. Current medications include omeprazole and loratadine. Comprehensive workup ordered with follow-up in 1 week.
rubric: Summary must mention: chief complaint, current medications, and follow-up plan.

input: Patient: Susan Park, 71F. CC: confusion and urinary urgency for 2 days. PMH: dementia (mild), hypertension, recurrent UTIs. Meds: donepezil 10mg QD, amlodipine 5mg QD, vitamin D 1000IU QD. UA: positive nitrites, leukocyte esterase 3+, WBC >50/hpf. Assessment: uncomplicated UTI with acute delirium overlay. Plan: nitrofurantoin 100mg BID x 5 days, encourage fluids, recheck mental status in 48 hours.
reference: Susan Park presents with confusion and urinary urgency consistent with UTI causing delirium. Medications include donepezil and amlodipine. Follow-up for mental status recheck is in 48 hours.
rubric: Summary must mention: chief complaint, current medications, and follow-up plan.

input: Patient: James Walker, 48M. CC: recurrent episodes of palpitations and dizziness over 4 weeks. PMH: anxiety disorder, hypothyroidism. Meds: sertraline 100mg QD, levothyroxine 75mcg QD. TSH normal. Holter monitor ordered for 2 weeks. Assessment: palpitations likely anxiety-related vs. arrhythmia; rule out cardiac cause. Plan: cardiology referral, avoid caffeine and alcohol, follow up with Holter results in 3 weeks.
reference: James Walker presents with recurrent palpitations and dizziness. Current medications include sertraline and levothyroxine. Holter monitor ordered and cardiology referral placed; follow-up in 3 weeks.
rubric: Summary must mention: chief complaint, current medications, and follow-up plan.

input: Patient: Emily Torres, 28F. CC: rash over bilateral forearms, pruritic, 1-week history. PMH: eczema. Meds: hydrocortisone cream 1% PRN, cetirizine 10mg QD. Exam: erythematous papular rash, no vesicles, no systemic symptoms. Assessment: contact dermatitis vs. atopic dermatitis flare. Plan: triamcinolone 0.1% cream BID for 7 days, patch testing referral, avoid known irritants, follow up in 2 weeks.
reference: Emily Torres presents with pruritic rash on bilateral forearms consistent with contact dermatitis. Medications include hydrocortisone cream and cetirizine. Follow-up in 2 weeks with patch testing referral.
rubric: Summary must mention: chief complaint, current medications, and follow-up plan.

input: Patient: Michael Johnson, 62M. CC: follow-up for poorly controlled type 2 diabetes. A1c today 9.2% (was 8.4% six months ago). Meds: metformin 1000mg BID, glipizide 10mg BID. No hypoglycemic episodes. Diet recall shows high carbohydrate intake. Plan: add semaglutide 0.25mg SC weekly, diabetes education referral, dietitian consultation, recheck A1c in 3 months.
reference: Michael Johnson has poorly controlled type 2 diabetes with rising A1c of 9.2%. Medications include metformin and glipizide. Semaglutide added and follow-up with A1c recheck in 3 months.
rubric: Summary must mention: chief complaint, current medications, and follow-up plan.

input: Patient: Alice Brown, 39F. Diagnosis: community-acquired pneumonia, moderate severity. Started on amoxicillin-clavulanate 875mg BID for 7 days and azithromycin 500mg QD for 5 days. Allergies: penicillin (rash). Follow-up date: July 15, 2025. SpO2 92% on RA — admitted for observation.
